Container.Name: claude-code-cli
Container.Overview:
Vanilla, non-root Anthropic Claude Code CLI - nothing added except two explicit, optional pieces: a browser-terminal link (ttyd) that drops you straight into an authenticated `claude` session, and an optional built-in HTTP bridge (cc-bridge) for programmatic/chat-frontend access (e.g. a Telegram bot). No dashboard, no message bus, no orchestration daemon, no forced auto-update.
WebUI link: opens a real terminal running `claude` directly in your browser - the same as `docker exec -it claude-code claude` - not a general-purpose shell and not a chat UI.
cc-bridge (separate port, not for direct browser use): POST /chat {"message","model"} runs `claude -p --model <model>` as a subprocess and returns its reply; conversation history persists across restarts under CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR in your choice of sqlite/markdown/json (CC_BRIDGE_STORAGE variable below); DELETE /session clears history; GET /health checks `claude` is on PATH. 120s subprocess timeout - a timed-out call returns an error but does not touch the session, so the next message continues normally.
First-time setup: open the WebUI link once and run `claude login` (or set an API key - see README) before using cc-bridge.
Container.Description: Vanilla, non-root Claude Code CLI plus an optional browser-terminal WebUI (ttyd, running `claude` directly) and an optional cc-bridge HTTP API for programmatic/chat-frontend access. No dashboard, no message bus, no daemon beyond those two explicit pieces.

Config.Name: AppData (login + settings)
Config.Description: This is what makes your `claude login` session, MCP servers, and installed plugins survive a container recreate or image update. Points at CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR inside the container - do not remove this volume unless you want to lose your login.

Config.Name: Workspace
Config.Description: Working directory for `claude` sessions and cc-bridge. The appdata path below is just a starting default - for real project work, change this to point at an actual share (e.g. /mnt/user/projects/my-project) instead.

Config.Name: WebUI port (ttyd)
Config.Description: Browser terminal. Opening this in a browser lands you directly inside a live `claude` session, already authenticated via the AppData volume above - equivalent to running `docker exec -it claude-code claude` yourself. Not a dashboard.

Config.Name: cc-bridge port
Config.Description: HTTP API for programmatic/chat-frontend access (e.g. a Telegram bot calling this container). Endpoints: POST /chat, DELETE /session, GET /health. Not meant for direct browser use - point automation at this port, point yourself at the WebUI port above.

Config.Name: CC_BRIDGE_STORAGE
Config.Description: cc-bridge conversation history backend. sqlite (default): compact single-file DB, best for large histories. markdown: single human-readable .md file, easiest to read/edit by hand. json: single .json file, easiest to parse from other scripts. All three persist under the AppData volume and survive restarts - this only controls the file format.

Config.Name: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (optional)
Config.Description: Optional. Lets cc-bridge's `claude -p` subprocess calls authenticate without an interactive login. Leave blank if you've already run `claude login` once via the WebUI - that session persists in the AppData volume and works just as well.
